result.markdown:
--- title: "The golden path" subtitle: "The whole workflow — data to group — with recovered numbers and a parity check." jupyter: python3 execute: freeze: auto --- Every other tutorial shows one part of the workflow. This page runs the **whole** thing — `fmri_dataset → fmri_lm → contrast → group` — without a break, on data with a *known* injected effect, then checks fmrimod's first-level math against the Nilearn reference using a saved comparison file. Nothing here asks you to trust the prose: the recovered numbers and the agreement with Nilearn are on the page. ## The helper that moves results to the group level `group_dataset_from_contrasts` takes the per-subject contrast results and bundles them into one group dataset: ::: {#67049e7d .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} from fmrimod import group_dataset_from_contrasts ``` ::: ## A dataset with a known effect We simulate sixteen subjects. Each has a true `face − scene` effect drawn from `N(2.0, 0.6)` in five of ten voxels; the other five are null. The signal is placed by the **typed column identity** — we ask the compiled model which column is `term="condition", level="face"` — never a hand-typed column string. ::: {#358ce06f .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} import numpy as np import pandas as pd import fmrimod as fm from fmrimod.spec import hrf, drift from fmrimod.contrast import condition from fmrimod.group import ols_voxelwise N_SUB = 16 spec = hrf("condition", basis="spmg1") + drift("poly", degree=2) events = pd.DataFrame({ "onset": np.arange(8, 210, 12.0), "condition": np.where(np.arange(17) % 2 == 0, "face", "scene"), "duration": 2.0, }) ``` ::: ::: {.callout-note collapse="true"} ## `make_demo_dataset` (skim — test-data plumbing, not the lesson) Builds the same design `fmri_lm` would (via `compile`), places a known effect on the `face` regressor found *by name* through the column map, then uses fmrimod's own [`simulate_bold`](simulation.qmd) to make a noisy series. The same helper appears in [get started](../get-started.qmd). ::: {#88939f44 .cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} from fmrimod.spec import compile as compile_spec from fmrimod.design_colmap import design_colmap from fmrimod.simulate import simulate_bold def make_demo_dataset(model_spec, events, effect, term="condition", n_signal=5, n_voxels=10, noise=1.5, seed=0): sampling = fm.SamplingFrame(blocklens=220, TR=1.0) built, _ = compile_spec(model_spec, data=events, sampling_frame=sampling) face_col = design_colmap(built).query( f"condition == '{term}.face'" ).index[0] true_betas = np.zeros((built.design_matrix.shape[1], n_voxels)) true_betas[face_col, :n_signal] = effect # the known effect bold = simulate_bold( np.asarray(built.design_matrix), true_betas, noise_sd=noise, n_voxels=n_voxels, rng=np.random.default_rng(seed), ) return fm.fmri_dataset(bold, tr=1.0, events=events) ``` ::: ::: ## Stage 1–3: dataset → fit → typed contrast For one subject: fit the model, then state the hypothesis by **naming conditions**. The recovered estimate returns the injected `2.0` in the signal voxels and ~0 in the null voxels — the typed contrast is *right*, not merely runnable. ::: {#e4983063 .cell execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code} fit = fm.fmri_lm(spec, make_demo_dataset(spec, events, effect=2.0, seed=202)) con = fit.contrast( condition("face", term="condition") - condition("scene", term="condition"), name="face_minus_scene", ) pd.DataFrame({ "estimate": con.estimate.round(3), "t": con.stat.round(2), "p": con.p_value.round(4), }) # voxels 0–4 ≈ 2.0 at p≈0; voxels 5–9 null ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=4} ```{=html}
estimate t p
0 2.041 71.67 0.0000
1 1.970 61.91 0.0000
2 1.964 66.19 0.0000
3 1.980 67.08 0.0000
4 1.980 63.96 0.0000
5 0.044 1.48 0.1405
6 0.024 0.77 0.4411
7 -0.046 -1.48 0.1391
8 0.048 1.62 0.1078
9 -0.042 -1.36 0.1745
``` ::: ::: ## Stage 4: contrasts → group `group_dataset_from_contrasts` takes the per-subject `ContrastResult` objects from stage 3 directly — no hand-built second-level table, no restart from synthetic betas. `ols_voxelwise` then fits the group model and the recovered group estimate returns the **true between-subject mean**. ::: {#424f6ca8 .cell execution_count=6} ``` {.python .cell-code} master = np.random.default_rng(2) subject_effect = master.normal(2.0, 0.6, N_SUB) per_subject = { f"sub-{i:02d}": fm.fmri_lm( spec, make_demo_dataset(spec, events, effect=subject_effect[i], seed=200 + i) ).contrast( condition("face", term="condition") - condition("scene", term="condition"), name="face_minus_scene", ) for i in range(N_SUB) } gd = group_dataset_from_contrasts(per_subject) group = ols_voxelwise(gd, formula="~ 1") pd.DataFrame({ "group_estimate": np.asarray(group.assay("coef:Intercept")).ravel().round(3), "t": np.asarray(group.assay("t_coef:Intercept")).ravel().round(2), "p": np.asarray(group.assay("p_coef:Intercept")).ravel().round(4), }) # voxels 0–4 recover the true group mean; voxels 5–9 stay null ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=5} ```{=html}
group_estimate t p
0 2.002 13.86 0.0000
1 2.000 13.52 0.0000
2 1.997 13.51 0.0000
3 1.992 13.65 0.0000
4 1.981 13.73 0.0000
5 -0.007 -0.74 0.4692
6 0.004 0.57 0.5776
7 0.009 1.00 0.3315
8 0.001 0.10 0.9234
9 -0.002 -0.37 0.7180
``` ::: ::: ::: {#ebd8e0f3 .cell execution_count=7} ``` {.python .cell-code} f"true between-subject mean = {subject_effect.mean():.3f}" ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=6} ``` 'true between-subject mean = 1.994' ``` ::: ::: The group estimate in the signal voxels matches the true mean to three decimals, with every signal voxel significant and every null voxel not — the workflow runs end to end on real numbers, not array shapes. ## The parity receipt Recovering an injected effect shows the workflow agrees with *itself*. It does not show the first-level math matches an independent reference. For that we load a **saved comparison file** — fmrimod's public F-contrast / confound / drift workflow run against Nilearn's GLM — and read its agreement numbers straight onto the page. ::: {#310bb766 .cell execution_count=8} ``` {.python .cell-code} import json from pathlib import Path root = next(c for c in [Path.cwd(), *Path.cwd().parents] if (c / "benchmarks" / "parity").is_dir()) report = json.loads( (root / "benchmarks/parity/tier_a_f_confound_drift/reports_public/parity_report.json").read_text() ) print(f"status: {report['status']}") print(f"caveats: {report['caveats'] or 'none'}") print(f"runtime: {report['timings']['seconds']:.3f} s") pd.DataFrame([ {"quantity": k, "max |fmrimod − Nilearn|": f"{v['max_abs']:.2e}", "gate": v["gate"]} for k, v in report["deltas"].items() ]) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` status: pass caveats: none runtime: 0.370 s ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=7} ```{=html}
quantity max |fmrimod − Nilearn| gate
0 design 0.00e+00 allclose+pearson+spearman
1 effect_condition_a_minus_b 2.66e-15 allclose+pearson+spearman
2 f_conditions_omnibus 3.68e-05 allclose+pearson+spearman
3 t_condition_a_minus_b 2.69e-08 allclose+pearson+spearman
``` ::: ::: fmrimod agrees with Nilearn to `~1e-5` on the omnibus *F*, and to `~1e-8` or tighter on the effect, *t*, and design — with the standard gate green and no active caveat. A skeptic does not have to take the mission statement's word for it; the artifact is in the repo and is regenerated by the parity suite. ::: {.callout-tip} ## What fmrimod expressed more directly The hypothesis on this page is the single line `condition("face", term="condition") - condition("scene", term="condition")`. That object *is* the scientific question, and it carried its own identity from the first-level fit into the group stage untouched. The Nilearn/SPM equivalent is a positional contrast vector the user hand-builds against a design-matrix column order they must first reconstruct, then re-aligns by hand at the group level. fmrimod did not match the reference *despite* being typed — the typed contrast is *why* the step from first level to group needed no manual column bookkeeping. ::: ## Scope, honestly This page ends the workflow with the fixed-effects `ols_voxelwise` reduction to keep the group step to one line. That is a deliberate scope choice, not a capability limit: the typed **random-effects** path also handles a covariate formula — `fmri_meta(..., formula="~ 1 + age", method="dl")` is a parity port of the R oracle `fmrigds` `meta:re_reg`. The one honest edge worth stating: `method="pm"` and `method="reml"` stay intercept-only by design (there is no R oracle to port a `pm`/`reml` regression against), so a covariate formula with those methods raises `NotImplementedError` deliberately rather than guessing. [Group analysis](group-analysis.qmd) demonstrates the random-effects meta-regression end to end. ## Next - [Group analysis](group-analysis.qmd) — the typed second-level interface in depth - [Contrasts](contrasts.qmd) — the typed contrast algebra - [Benchmark datasets](benchmark-datasets.qmd) — the parity artifacts behind the receipt
